name: Varo
description_short: First consumer fintech to receive a national bank charter. Fee-free checking, up to 5% APY savings, cash advances up to $250, no credit check. FDIC insured directly.
description_long:
Varo Bank is a San Francisco-based digital bank founded in 2015 that made history in 2020 by becoming the first consumer fintech to receive a national bank charter from the OCC. Unlike Chime and Dave which partner with traditional banks, Varo IS a bank — your deposits are directly FDIC insured.

Varo Checking: Fee-free with no minimum balance, early direct deposit (up to 2 days), and a Visa debit card. No overdraft fees.

Varo Advance: Cash advances from $20 to $250 with no interest. Available instantly to your Varo account. Repaid automatically from your next deposit. No credit check required. Eligibility increases with consistent direct deposit history.

Varo Savings: Up to 5.00% APY on balances up to $5,000 (requires qualifying direct deposit), one of the highest savings rates available. No minimum balance, no monthly fees.

Varo Believe: A secured credit card that helps you build credit. Load money, spend it, and Varo reports to all three bureaus. No credit check, no interest, no annual fee — similar to Chime's Credit Builder.

Varo is particularly well-suited for people who've been denied by traditional banks. There's no ChexSystems check, no minimum balance, and no monthly fees. The combination of fee-free banking, cash advances, high-yield savings, and credit building makes it a comprehensive financial platform for underserved consumers.
pros: Actual bank (national charter) — FDIC insured directly
Cash advances up to $250, no interest
Credit builder card reports to all 3 bureaus
No ChexSystems check — true second chance banking
cons: Cash advances start small, grow with history
Relatively new as a chartered bank (2020)
App-only experience may not suit everyone
